marketFocus: Trump told aides he's willing to end the war WITHOUT reopening Hormuz — oil whipsawed (WTI settled $102.88, Brent $112.78, off highs). Iran denies all negotiations. Overnight strikes continued: Isfahan ammo depot hit, Shiraz Airport explosions, Saudi Arabia intercepted 4 ballistic missiles toward Riyadh, Kuwaiti crude carrier struck in Dubai port. Risk tone: Mixed.

German CPI Prelim BEAT: 2.7% YoY (vs 2.3% exp, prev 1.9%) — energy prices +7.2% YoY, first increase since Dec 2023. Eurozone CPI Flash y/y at 11:00 FFT is today's critical event — a hot print reinforces the ECB two-hike narrative. ECB Stournaras: 'will react to second-round effects; stagflation more likely if conflict protracted.'

Chinese NBS Manufacturing PMI BEAT: 50.4 (vs 50.0 exp). Tokyo CPI MISS: 1.4% (vs 1.7% exp). RBA Minutes: board divided on whether to hike — 'oil at $100 would lift CPI to ~5% in June quarter.' UK Final GDP Q4 at 08:00 FFT. JOLTS + CB Consumer Confidence at 16:00 FFT.

USD.tone: DXY Marginally Softer but Firm — JOLTS + Consumer Confidence at 16:00 FFT Key
USD.summary: USD is Bullish today. DXY marginally softened after gaining yesterday alongside higher oil prices and geopolitical uncertainty — mixed signals from Trump (willing to end war without reopening Hormuz, but also threatened to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants if no deal). Fed Williams said uncertainty around inflation path is 'high' and tariffs + Iran war will push up headline inflation, but added the economy has been more resilient than expected. Monetary policy is 'appropriately positioned.' JOLTS Job Openings and CB Consumer Confidence at 16:00 FFT are the key catalysts today. Quarter-end rebalancing flows also active. Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: DXY marginally softer but still firm — mixed geopolitical signals keep dollar supported
Fed Williams: 'uncertainty around inflation path is high; tariffs + Iran war push up inflation'
Fed Williams: 'economy more resilient than expected; monetary policy appropriately positioned'
JOLTS Job Openings + CB Consumer Confidence at 15:00 FFT — key catalysts today
Quarter-end rebalancing flows active — USD demand from institutional rebalancing

EUR.tone: German CPI 2.7% YoY Beat — Eurozone CPI Flash at 11:00 FFT is the Critical Event
EUR.summary: EUR is Bullish today. German CPI Prelim beat strongly: 2.7% YoY (vs 2.3% exp, prev 1.9%) — energy prices +7.2% YoY, first increase since December 2023. EUR/USD clawed back above 1.1500 after slipping below yesterday. ECB Stournaras said the ECB would react to second-round price effects and warned that a protracted Middle East conflict makes stagflation more likely. ECB Lane said 'no paralysis on potential rate moves.' The Eurozone CPI Flash at 11:00 FFT is today's critical event — a hot print (above 2.5% YoY) would cement the ECB two-hike narrative and push EUR significantly higher. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: German CPI Prelim BEAT: 2.7% YoY (vs 2.3% exp) — energy prices +7.2% YoY first rise since Dec 2023
ECB Stournaras: 'will react to second-round effects; stagflation more likely if conflict protracted'
ECB Lane: 'no paralysis on potential rate moves' — hawkish tone intact
EUR/USD clawed back above 1.1500 — rebound contained ahead of Eurozone CPI Flash
Eurozone CPI Flash y/y at 11:00 FFT — CRITICAL event; hot print cements ECB two-hike story

GBP.tone: UK Final GDP Q4 at 08:00 FFT — GBP/USD Returned to 1.3200, BOE Repricing Intact
GBP.summary: GBP is Neutral today. GBP/USD found some reprieve from recent selling and returned to the 1.3200 territory. UK mortgage approvals beat (62.58K vs 61K exp) and UK M4 Money Supply beat (0.6% vs 0.1% exp) — positive domestic credit signals. UK Final GDP Q4 at 08:00 FFT is the key event — a revision higher would give GBP a fresh catalyst. BOE repricing (~81bps tightening priced) remains intact but needs fresh data to drive GBP directionally. ECB Stournaras warning about stagflation applies equally to the UK context. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: UK Final GDP Q4 at 08:00 FFT — key event; revision higher would give GBP fresh catalyst
UK Mortgage Approvals BEAT: 62.58K vs 61K exp — positive domestic credit signal
UK M4 Money Supply BEAT: 0.6% MoM vs 0.1% exp — credit expansion intact
GBP/USD returned to 1.3200 — reprieve from recent selling pressure
BOE repricing (~81bps tightening priced) intact but needs fresh catalyst to drive GBP

JPY.tone: Tokyo CPI Miss + Retail Sales Miss — Verbal Intervention Losing Credibility
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. Tokyo CPI missed: 1.4% YoY (vs 1.7% exp, prev 1.6%). Tokyo Core CPI missed: 1.7% YoY (vs 1.8% exp). Japanese Retail Sales missed badly: -0.2% YoY (vs +0.8% exp, prev +1.8%). USD/JPY was choppy amid the mixed geopolitical headlines and overnight oil fluctuations. The BoJ Summary of Opinions from Monday was hawkish (debated need for more hikes, mentioned 'falling behind the curve'), but the data flow today is clearly disappointing — domestic demand is weakening. Verbal intervention from Mimura, Ueda and Katayama pushed USD/JPY lower yesterday but the effect is fading. Tankan Large Manufacturing (overnight, 00:50 FFT) is the other key event. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Tokyo CPI MISS: 1.4% YoY (vs 1.7% exp) — inflation undershooting expectations
Tokyo Core CPI MISS: 1.7% YoY (vs 1.8% exp) — energy shock not yet feeding through
Japanese Retail Sales MISS: -0.2% YoY (vs +0.8% exp) — domestic demand weakening
BoJ SOO hawkish but data disappoints — verbal intervention losing credibility
USD/JPY choppy; Japan 2yr JGB auction mixed (b/c 3.54x, yield 1.370% vs prev 1.244%)

AUD.tone: RBA Minutes Divided — Oil at $100 Would Lift CPI to 5% in June Quarter
AUD.summary: AUD is Bearish today. RBA Minutes from March meeting revealed a divided board: majority agreed further tightening was needed and financial conditions were 'insufficiently restrictive, even accommodative.' However, a minority wanted to wait for more information, and the board agreed 'it is not possible to predict the future path of rates with any confidence given the Middle East conflict.' Critically, the RBA noted that oil prices around $100/bbl would lift annual CPI inflation to ~5% in the June quarter — a stagflation signal. Antipodeans were rangebound and failed to benefit from the China PMI beat. Australia's inflation expectations rose to a record 7.3% (ANZ Roy Morgan). ASX 200 rallied but AUD could not sustain gains. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: RBA Minutes: board divided — majority hawkish but minority wants to wait; no clear path
RBA: 'oil at $100 would lift annual CPI to ~5% in June quarter' — stagflation risk
Australia inflation expectations hit record 7.3% (ANZ Roy Morgan) — demand-pull building
Antipodeans rangebound; failed to benefit from China NBS Manufacturing PMI beat (50.4)
ASX 200 rallied but AUD rangebound — equity strength not translating to currency

CAD.tone: WTI Settled $102.88 + Kuwait Carrier Struck in Dubai Port — Oil Supply Disruption Intact
CAD.summary: CAD is Bullish today. WTI settled at $102.88/bbl and Brent at $112.78/bbl despite Trump's end-war signal — the oil supply disruption is too severe to reverse on a headline. Iran struck a Kuwaiti crude carrier in Dubai's port (Kuwait warned of potential oil spill, later contained). Iraq Kirkuk oil exports to resume via Turkey-Ceyhan pipeline — a modest supply positive but insufficient to offset Hormuz closure. SocGen sees Brent averaging $125/bbl in April and a growing likelihood of $150/bbl. US Treasury Secretary Bessent: 'oil market is in a deficit of 10-12mln barrels a day.' No major CAD data today — pure oil proxy. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: WTI settled $102.88/bbl, Brent $112.78/bbl — oil elevated despite Trump end-war signal
Iran struck Kuwaiti crude carrier in Dubai port — supply chain disruption intensifying
Bessent: 'oil market in deficit of 10-12mln barrels/day' — supply gap confirmed
SocGen: Brent to average $125/bbl in April; growing likelihood of $150/bbl
Iraq Kirkuk exports to resume via Turkey-Ceyhan pipeline — modest supply positive

CHF.tone: Haven Flows to USD — SNB Intervention Cap + KOF Miss Yesterday Weigh on CHF
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. Haven flows continue to favour USD over CHF due to the yield advantage. Swiss KOF Leading Indicators missed badly yesterday (96.1 vs 102 exp) — growth concerns are building. ECB Stournaras warning about stagflation in the Eurozone applies to Switzerland's export-dependent economy. SNB intervention cap remains intact, limiting CHF appreciation. No Swiss data today. EUR/CHF is stabilizing near the 0.9100 area. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: Haven flows favour USD over CHF — yield advantage keeps dollar preferred in risk-off
Swiss KOF Leading Indicators missed badly yesterday: 96.1 vs 102 exp — growth concerns
SNB intervention cap limits CHF appreciation; sight deposits rose modestly last week
ECB Stournaras stagflation warning applies to Switzerland's export-dependent economy
No Swiss data today — CHF trading purely on global risk sentiment and USD dynamics

NZD.tone: Antipodeans Rangebound — Failed to Benefit from China PMI Beat, No Domestic Catalyst
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today. Antipodeans were rangebound and failed to benefit from the stronger-than-expected Chinese NBS Manufacturing PMI (50.4 vs 50.0 exp) and Non-Manufacturing PMI (50.1 vs 49.9 exp). The China PMI beat is a positive for NZD via the trade channel, but the geopolitical uncertainty and risk-off tone are overwhelming the data signal. PBoC set a slightly stronger yuan fix (6.9194 vs exp 6.9209, prev 6.9223) — marginally positive but not enough to drive NZD. No domestic NZD catalyst today. RBNZ stagflation warning intact. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: Antipodeans rangebound; failed to benefit from China NBS PMI beat (Mfg 50.4, Non-Mfg 50.1)
PBoC set slightly stronger yuan fix (6.9194 vs exp 6.9209) — marginal positive, not enough
RBNZ stagflation warning intact: energy shock raises inflation while weighing on growth
No domestic NZD catalyst today — pure risk sentiment proxy
Geopolitical uncertainty and risk-off tone overwhelming positive China data signal
